testRoute: Playwright browser automation with static HTML test page (file:// protocol)
frameCheckMethod: ffmpeg extraction at 30fps + per-frame file-size analysis for blank frames + exact buffer comparison for duplicates

blankFrames.analysis: Initial pre-render frames (985 bytes each). Expected behavior — page content not yet painted.

duplicateFrames.analysis: Adjacent duplicate frames occur when scroll step (60px) is smaller than row height (~80-120px). This is expected and NOT a visual anomaly — the viewport simply hasn't changed between frames.

caveats: Test used static HTML simulation, not the actual React ChatView component. The real ChatView has additional complexity: useStableChatScroll hook, useChatMessages state management, timeline store subscriptions, and older-history auto-loading.
The actual ChatView renders ALL messages in DOM (no virtualization). With 2000 messages this creates a 383KB scroll height. Performance may degrade on lower-end devices.
No streaming-like updates were tested. The scroll stability during live message streaming (assistant text appearing incrementally) was not verified.
No older-history prepend was tested. The loadOlderWithoutJump + scroll anchor restoration logic was not exercised.

scrollArchitecture.useStableChatScroll: Uses useLayoutEffect + requestAnimationFrame triple-settle for initial bottom scroll. Tracks isAtBottomRef for conditional scrollToBottom.
scrollArchitecture.chatHistoryAutoLoad: Implements threshold-based auto-loading with rearm distance. Uses scrollTop/scrollHeight ratios.
scrollArchitecture.chatScrollDebug: Conditional debug logging via localStorage flag. Safe no-op when disabled.
scrollArchitecture.chatStableIds: Uses fingerprint-based stable IDs with reverse occurrence counting for duplicate user messages. Merges assistant turns via coalesce option.

potentialIssues: ChatView renders ALL messages in DOM (no virtualization). With 10k+ messages this could cause performance issues.
useStableChatScroll.settleAtBottom uses triple requestAnimationFrame + setTimeout. Could cause scroll jank if layout thrashing occurs.
loadOlderWithoutJump captures scroll anchor before load, restores after render. If render takes >1 frame, user may see content shift.
handleScroll callback runs on every scroll event. Includes shouldAutoLoadOlderHistory check which could be throttled.
No IntersectionObserver used for visible row tracking. All rows are always in DOM.
